frameworks.name: Hodgkin-Huxley single-compartment conductance model
frameworks.one_line: single-compartment DA neuron with voltage- and Ca2+-dependent currents

frameworks.name: homeostatic vs hedonic regulation of feeding
frameworks.one_line: feeding controlled by two systems; hedonic role of midbrain DA examined

frameworks.name: pacemaker firing of midbrain dopaminergic neurons
frameworks.one_line: model extended to capture irregular firing seen after high-fat diet

core_question: Does diet-induced obesity alter the spontaneous firing patterns of midbrain dopaminergic neurons, and what biophysical mechanisms underlie this change?
key_assumptions: DA firing patterns in vitro reflect intrinsic biophysical mechanisms
voltage- and Ca2+-dependent currents suffice to capture firing patterns
high-fat diet feeding can modify DA neuron membrane properties
manipulations: diet condition (high-fat vs normal chow) in mice
voltage-clamp-derived current parameters in the model
model parameters distinguishing regular vs irregular firing
key_findings: DA neurons usually show regular pacemaker-like firing in slices
high-fat-diet mice show increased proportion of irregularly firing DA neurons
new voltage-clamp data from HFD and NCD slices constrain the model
interpretations: hedonic-system DA activity, not only homeostatic circuits, is altered by diet-induced obesity
biophysical model can account for diet-related shifts in DA firing patterns
